tech_name: Lawson Process Suites (Financials, HR, Procurement, Supply Chain)
notes: Four horizontal suites replacing functional silos; core of process-centric strategy
Lawson Insight implemented ABC via intelligent data warehouse; synchronized with GL but unconstrained by GL metadata model
In second release by May 1997; full BMS functionality from web browser; Insight screens as JAVA forms
6 pre-built web apps: Employee, Supervisor, Internal Procurement, Customer Information, Vendor Information, Financial
Repository maintaining business object methods shared across application suites; enables process-centric architecture
Distributed computing standard; cited by Lawson and AWG as key internet enabling technology
Lawson WebDesktop delivered Insight screens as JAVA forms; Phase II planned JAVA Server support
Lawson had 2300+ customers on AS/400, Unix, and Windows NT; AS/400 renamed IBM i; still active
